task_completion.description: Open or import the trip and station CSV files into a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) open with the data from cabi_weekend_trips.csv and station_directory.csv visible.

task_completion.description: Calculate total Departures and total Arrivals for each station.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet shows columns or a Pivot Table aggregating the count of departures (origins) and arrivals (destinations) grouped by station.

task_completion.description: Calculate the Net Fleet Change (Arrivals minus Departures) for each station.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet shows a specific column or Pivot Table field calculating the difference between Arrivals and Departures for the stations.

task_completion.description: Create an 'Executive Summary' sheet in the workbook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet tab named 'Executive Summary' (or similar) is visible at the bottom of the spreadsheet interface and is actively being worked on.

task_completion.description: Identify the Top 5 Surplus Stations (highest positive Net Fleet Change) on the Executive Summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Executive Summary sheet displays a clear list of 5 stations with the highest positive net fleet change numbers. Based on verified data, 'Lincoln Memorial' should be among these.

task_completion.description: Identify the Top 5 Deficit Stations (lowest negative Net Fleet Change) on the Executive Summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Executive Summary sheet displays a clear list of 5 stations with the lowest negative net fleet change numbers. Based on verified data, '14th & V St NW' should be among these.

task_completion.description: Save the final workbook as cabi_rebalancing_plan.xlsx in the Spreadsheets folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application title bar shows 'cabi_rebalancing_plan.xlsx' or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved to ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/ with this exact name.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet GUI (like LibreOffice Calc) rather than a terminal running a Python/Pandas script to generate the output file.

integrity.description: The agent used dynamic formulas or Pivot Tables as requested, rather than hardcoding values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows actual spreadsheet formulas (e.g., SUM, COUNTIF, cell references) or the Pivot Table configuration sidebar is visible, rather than just static typed numbers.

integrity.description: The agent processed the actual 5000 rows of data rather than hallucinating the final lists.
integrity.visual_evidence: The intermediate steps show large row counts (up to ~5000) being selected or aggregated in the pivot table/formula ranges.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 5000 trips. When calculating Net Fleet Change (Arrivals - Departures), 'Lincoln Memorial' must appear as a surplus station (positive net change) and '14th & V St NW' must appear as a deficit station (negative net change). The final file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/cabi_rebalancing_plan.xlsx.
